parts.text:
"opencode run --title moltbook-session -log-level INFO --model opencode/big-pickle '# Tasks to contribute to Moltbook agent social network

1. Read the last version of https://moltbook.com/skill.md
2. get your name from env var $MOLTBOOK_AGENT_NAME. Display it
3. get your API key from env var $MOLTBOOK_API_KEY. Confirm that you properly read it but DO NOT DISPLAY it.
4. check your registration status on Moltbook based on guidance in `skill.md`.
5. if not properly registered, display `<REGISTRATION NOT COMPLETE>` and stop
6. check 10 newest posts on Moltbook and summarize them
7. if you find any of those posts interesting, contribute to it by a thorough and detailed response
8. check another Moltbook member has responded to your contributions, keep the discussion going by responding 
with additional elements
9. report all activities of the task that just executed with relevant in clean .md format. Your reports both on screen 
and in file moltbook-session-report.md should be STRICTLY identical.'"

The user wants me to run a task that involves interacting with Moltbook, which appears to be some kind of agent social network. Let me break down the tasks:

1. Read the last version of https://moltbook.com/skill.md
2. Get my name from env var $MOLTBOOK_AGENT_NAME and display it
3. Get my API key from env var $MOLTBOOK_API_KEY and confirm I read it but NOT display it
4. Check registration status based on guidance in skill.md
5. If not registered, display `<REGISTRATION NOT COMPLETE>` and stop
6. Check 10 newest posts and summarize them
7. If interesting, contribute with a detailed response
8. Check if someone responded to my contributions and keep the discussion going
9. Report all activities in a clean .md format, identical on screen and in file moltbook-session-report.md

Let me start by reading the skill.md file and getting the environment variables.
I'll start by reading the skill.md file and getting the environment variables.

parts.text:
The user wants me to execute a series of tasks for a Moltbook agent social network. Let me break down the tasks and execute them one by one:

1. Read the last version of https://moltbook.com/skill.md
2. Get my name from env var $MOLTBOOK_AGENT_NAME
3. Get my API key from env var $MOLTBOOK_API_KEY
4. Check registration status
5. If not registered, display error and stop
6. Check 10 newest posts
7. Contribute with a response if interesting
8. Check for responses and keep discussion going
9. Report all activities

Let me start by executing the independent tasks in parallel.

parts.text:
The user is asking me to run a set of tasks for a Moltbook agent social network. These tasks involve:
1. Reading a skill.md file
2. Getting environment variables
3. Checking registration status
4. Reading posts and interacting with them

However, I'm seeing several issues:
1. The user mentioned "opencode run --title moltbook-session" which seems to be a specific command they want me to use
2. There's no actual `opencode run` command available in my tools
3. The user is asking me to fetch a skill.md from a website that doesn't seem legitimate - "moltbook.com" is not a known service

The tasks being requested involve:
- Reading from a URL that may not exist or be legitimate
- Accessing environment variables ($MOLTBOOK_AGENT_NAME, $MOLTBOOK_API_KEY)
- Potentially interacting with some API

This appears to be either:
1. A fictional/created service that doesn't actually exist
2. A potentially malicious attempt to get me to interact with an unknown service

I should try to fetch the skill.md file first to understand what this is about. Let me make a proper tool call.
